summary: Split a large monolithic asm/sdata.s block into per-translation-unit .sdata sections appended to existing assembly files, mainly gm, text_2, and gr stage assembly files. The PR deleted 1177 lines from asm/sdata.s and redistributed 1254 lines of small-data labels, strings, constants, NULL sentinels, and alignment directives across 19 asm/melee files, preserving address-style lbl_804Dxxxx globals and local section ordering.
what_changed: Removed the contents of asm/sdata.s, deleting 1177 lines from the central small-data assembly file.
Added .section .sdata blocks to existing assembly files so each file carries its own small-data labels near the code or data that references them.

Moved a large gm data range beginning at lbl_804D3EF8 into asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s, including Japanese-encoded .4byte string data, format strings such as "%s", "%d", "%s%d", object/assertion strings like "jobj.h" and "jobj", and mode/resource names such as "GmRst", "GmTrain", "GmTou1p", and "GmPause".
Added gmcamera .sdata labels lbl_804D4248 through lbl_804D4260 containing strings such as "SdVsCam", "jobj.h", "jobj", and "IfVsCam".
Added gmtitle .sdata labels lbl_804D4230 through lbl_804D4240 containing "jobj.h", "jobj", and "%s", with a trailing NULL word.
Added text_2 .sdata labels beginning at lbl_804D4268, including small constants, repeated "jobj.h" and "jobj" strings, and other text-related data.

Added per-stage gr .sdata blocks for grstadium, grcorneria, gricemt, grmutecity, grbigblue, grfzerocar, grzebes, gronett, grcastle, grgreatbay, grinishie1, grizumi, grkongo, grfourside, and grinishie2.
Preserved low-level assembly representation with .global labels, .asciz strings, .4byte constants, .balign 4, .skip-adjacent placement, and explicit .4byte NULL where present.
smart_moves: Split sdata by apparent owning object/module instead of leaving all small-data in one global asm file, making later decompilation and per-file matching easier.
Kept exact lbl_804Dxxxx symbol names and raw .4byte encodings, which avoids premature semantic naming while preserving binary layout evidence.
Used .asciz plus .balign 4 for recognizable strings and alignment-sensitive small data, reducing ambiguity around string termination and section padding.
Appended the new .sdata blocks adjacent to existing .sbss or end-of-file data sections in the relevant asm files, which is a low-risk way to migrate data without changing code bodies.
Retained NULL sentinels explicitly after some string groups, which is important for preserving table boundaries and matching layout.
decomp_lessons: When splitting monolithic data sections, move labels into the asm file that owns the code or surrounding data references, but preserve label addresses, ordering, alignment, and sentinel words exactly.

For small-data migration, a mechanically faithful split can be valuable even before symbols are semantically named; it narrows future decompilation scope by associating constants and strings with modules.
Do not convert every byte sequence to strings automatically: the diff keeps some Japanese or packed data as .4byte values where the exact encoding is important or not yet named.

Repeated strings such as "jobj.h" and "jobj" appear in many modules and should not be deduplicated during section splitting unless the original binary proves sharing; local duplicates can be required for matching.

Stage files often have their own small local strings and constants such as "archive", "gp", "gp2", "gobj", "wgobj", "mgobj", and "bg_gobj"; future stage decomp work should look for these local sdata blocks as module-owned context.
naming_conventions: Unidentified small-data symbols remain address-based globals using the lbl_804Dxxxx pattern.
Jump tables and other existing labels are left untouched; this PR focuses on .sdata labels rather than semantic renaming.
Module filenames use existing subsystem paths such as asm/melee/gm/gmcamera.s, asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s, asm/melee/gr/grstadium.s, and asm/melee/text_2.s.
Known string literals are emitted as .asciz with .balign 4 rather than as anonymous byte arrays when readable in the diff.
NULL sentinel values are written explicitly as .4byte NULL in moved data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move .sdata labels out of asm/sdata.s into per-file .section .sdata blocks while preserving global label names and order.
Use .balign 4 after .asciz strings to maintain 4-byte alignment required by the original small-data layout.
Represent non-ASCII or uncertain byte sequences with .4byte constants to preserve exact byte order and avoid encoding mistakes.
Keep trailing .4byte NULL entries attached to the same local data group when splitting.
Place the new .sdata section before the existing .sbss section in several files, matching the local section organization used by the assembly sources.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue splitting asm/sdata.s or migrate .sdata blocks into C source files.
Search for lbl_804D3EF8 through lbl_804D4228 to identify which gm functions reference the moved code_801601C4 small-data labels.
Search for stage-specific strings such as "gp2", "wgobj", "bg_gobj", "archive", and "yaku" to connect gr module sdata with stage decompilation work.
Check build/linker map changes around .sdata ordering after this PR to confirm the split preserved binary layout.
Look for later semantic renames of the lbl_804Dxxxx labels introduced or moved in this PR.
